title: fix commands without input
body:
in cases where commands require input and we dont provide it, we often would segv.  This can be attributed in many cases to the subcommand not picked up the cobra Args attribute or neither had them.

this includes eds pr #6054

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
LGTM. I don't understand the build error; the first thing I did was git-pr this locally and build it.

Probably one of the two commits does not build on its own; I'd try squashing them down to one.

LGTM otherwise.

>Probably one of the two commits does not build on its own

That seems impossible at first glance; and no matter how I look at it it's still impossible. The error message makes no sense. I'm starting to think there's something broken in the build_each_commit test.
Aha - `build_all_commits` is rebasing onto master, which now has #6047, which relies on `errors`. @baude, you need to rebase onto master and re-add the `pkg/errors` include.
Oops. Sorry, I meant to run the full BATS suite but got distracted.
